column221: I took marine biology in high school and it was my favorite class. I had the best teacher (Phil Tacata). I now am still involved with the marine biology program 8 years later at Petaluma High and chaperone their 3 day/overnight trip to Monterey where we go whale watching, go to año neuvo to observe elephant seals, and go to the Monterey bay aquarium and help educate high schoolers in the importance of our oceans and coastal wildlife. The ocean has always been my favorite place to be at. Always very healing and has helped my mental health a lot. My friends, family and I love to go to bodega bay, Tomales, and Point Reyes since they’re close to petaluma where we live. The ocean rocks!

column221:
I’m organizing the Central Sonoma Coast Firewise Community.  There are 433 private properties and Fort Ross, Salt Point Park, Stillwater Cove Park, and Kruse Rhododendron Park in the CSCFC.
The CSCFC lies along about 25 miles of coast land.

We are concerned about forests, coastal prairie and the ocean as an ecosystem.
There needs to be a facilitated governmental means to live here, take measures to protect from wildfire while re-establishing the ecological balance.
Now those of us owning land in the Coastal Zone run into prohibitive laws that keep us from reducing wildfire fuel.
To overcome these laws many of us cannot afford the legal requirements.
So… nothing is done. The forests are neglected. Prairies are overgrown with invasive species.  
The Coastal Commission should partner with local communities— not block our efforts.
